#  Search for and Apply Filters on the Subcontractor Invoices Tab 
#####  General Availability in Select Markets (United States)
![Flag Us](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/eb8f21ce49403a47d7bb5a0cedc7e0e9/7c0ec0977e654cc6bc9f3d8efa80fbe3/flag-us.png?fm=webp&h=14) [Procore Pay ](https://www.procore.com/pay) is available in the United States. It is designed for General Contractors and Owner-Builders who act as their own General Contractors on a job. Procore Pay extends the [Invoice Management](https://www.procore.com/invoice-management) functionality in the Procore web application to handle the payment process between general and specialty contractors.
##  Background 
The search feature helps you find an invoice or group of invoices that contain information matching your keyword search. You can also apply filters to narrow the items in view.
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/permissions)
  * **Additional Information** :
    * To learn about the different tabs in the Payments tool, see [About the Company Payments Tool](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/about-the-payments-tool).


##  Steps 
  * Search for Subcontractor Invoices
  * Apply Filters to the Data Table


### Search for Subcontractor Invoices
  1. Navigate to the Company level **Payments** tool.
  2. Click the **Subcontractor Invoices** tab.
  3. Enter keywords in the **Search** ![Icon Search](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/0e1287800ad9e8908deec3df3b65ebd0/c5fc5af3e51b77953edf62739fba7b2c/icon-search.png?fm=webp&h=15) field. Then press ENTER or press the TAB key to move the system's focus away from the **Search** field. 
#####  Tip
**What is the scope of the search?** Procore searches these data columns: **Invoice #** , **Beneficiary Name** , **Project** , and **Contract No**.
  
![Payments Search](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/91b0574f8152f2bdffc88b8c0e843567/f6c4e2b44ec40f7ef0312c6722d7770d/payments-search.png?fm=webp)


#####  Notes
  * If matches are found, Procore displays the matching items.
  * If no matches are found, Procore displays 'No Items Match Your Search' message.


### Apply Filters to the Data Table
  1. Navigate to the Company level **Payments** tool.
  2. Click the **Subcontractor Invoices** tab.
  3. Click **Filters** to open the Filter panel on the left side of the page. 
#####  Notes
     * To learn about the filters to apply to the data table in each tab, see [Subcontractor Invoices Tab: Default Columns](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/about-the-payments-tool) and [Disbursements Tab: Default Columns](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/about-the-payments-tool).
     * The illustration below shows you an example of the filters in the Subcontractor Invoices tab. The filters in other tabs are different.
![Subcontractor Invoice Filters1](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/51e2d3a66297533bc98bfcfe2e53ef25/483751bbfc3b99033a029c0c6b23c4bb/subcontractor-invoice-filters1.png?fm=webp)  

  4. Choose one or multiple filters:
     * **Invoice Status**  
Select one (1) or more statuses from the drop-down list. See [What are the default statuses for Procore invoices?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-the-default-statuses-for-procore-invoices)
     * **Payment Status**  
Select one (1) or more statuses from the drop-down list. See What are the default payment statuses in Procore Pay?
     * **Manual Holds**  
Select the _Applied_ or _Not Applied_ hold status from the drop-down list. See [Manage Payment Holds as a Payor](https://support.procore.com/products/online/user-guide/company-level/payments/tutorials/manage-payment-holds-as-a-payor).
     * **Beneficiary Name**  
Select one (1) or more beneficiaries from the drop-down list. These selections correspond to the 'Contract Company' selections on the project contracts in your company's Procore account. See [Create a Commitment](https://support.procore.com/products/online/user-guide/project-level/commitments/tutorials/create-a-commitment) and [Add a Company to the Project Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/set-up-a-project-directory/create-companies).
     * **Beneficiary Account Status**  
Select one (1) or more statuses from the drop-down list. These selections correspond to the onboarding statuses in Procore Pay. See What are the default statuses for Procore Pay onboarding?
     * **Project**  
Select one (1) or more projects from the drop-down list. These selections correspond to the active projects in your company's Procore account. See [Create a New Project](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/portfolio-company/tutorials/create-a-new-project).
     * **Billing Period**  
Select one (1) or more projects from the drop-down list. These selections correspond to the billing periods that have been created in the Project level Invoicing tool by your project's [invoice administrators](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms). See [Create Automatic Billing Periods](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/invoicing-project/tutorials/create-automatic-billing-periods) or [Create Manual Billing Periods](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/invoicing-project/tutorials/create-manual-billing-periods).
     * **Payment Due Date**  
Select one (1) filter: _Overdue_ , _Today_ , _Next 7 Days_ , or _Custom_ to choose a custom date range.
     * **Contract**  
Select one (1) or more contracts from the drop-down list. These selections correspond to the commitments created in the Project level Invoice tool on your company's Procore projects. See [Create a Commitment](https://support.procore.com/products/online/user-guide/project-level/commitments/tutorials/create-a-commitment).
     * **Contract Type**  
Select one (1) or more contract types from the drop-down list. These are Procore's default contract types. Select _Purchase Orders_ or _Subcontracts_.
  5. Press the TAB key or click an area outside the **Filters** panel to move the system focus. Procore refreshes the page and displays any content matching the filters you applied. 
#####  Tip
**Need to clear a filter?** To clear the table of all filters, click **Clear All Filters**. To remove a single filter, click the close (x) icon in the BLUE filter bubble.
  6. Click the **Close** icon (x) icon in the Filters panel. The selected filters remain applied when you close the pane.


## See Also
  * [About the Payments Tool](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/about-the-payments-tool)
  * [About the Subcontractor Invoices Tab in the Payments Tool](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/about-the-subcontractor-invoices-tab-in-the-payments-tool)
  * [Create Disbursements](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/create-disbursements)
title: Search for and Apply Filters on the Subcontractor Invoices Tab
description: To use the search feature and apply filters on the data tables in the different tabs of the Company level Payments tool.
